## The Matrix: Worked Example

Let `T = 2`, `d = 4`.

Input embedding (position-encoded):

```text
x = stream⁰[0] = [1.0, -0.5, 0.8, -0.2]
```

*LayerNorm step:*

```text
μ = (1.0 - 0.5 + 0.8 - 0.2) / 4 = 0.275
σ² = [(1.0-0.275)² + (-0.5-0.275)² + (0.8-0.275)² + (-0.2-0.275)²] / 4
   = 0.407
σ  = √0.407 ≈ 0.638

x_norm = [1.136, -1.215, 0.823, -0.745]
(With γ=1, β=0 for simplicity)
```

*MHA produces* (suppose): `mha_out = [0.3, 0.7, -0.1, 0.5]`

*Residual connection:*

```text
x₁ = x + mha_out = [1.0+0.3, -0.5+0.7, 0.8-0.1, -0.2+0.5]
   = [1.3, 0.2, 0.7, 0.3]
```

*Second LayerNorm + FFN + residual* (analogously) → $x_2$.

$x_1$ contains *both* the original information (from `x`) and the new information (from `mha_out`).
Nothing is overwritten — the residual stream accumulates.

@fig-ch10-matrix-block shows the full Pre-LN transformer block with LayerNorm before each sub-layer and residual connections around both.

```{dot}
//| label: fig-ch10-matrix-block
//| fig-cap: "Full transformer block (Pre-LN) — LayerNorm before each sub-layer, residual connections around both."
digraph transformer_block {
    rankdir=TB
    node [fontname="monospace", shape=box, style="rounded,filled", fillcolor=white, penwidth=2]
    edge [color="#555555"]

    x_in  [label="x  (input)",              shape=plaintext, style="", penwidth=1]
    ln1   [label="Layer Norm 1",             color="#1177BB", fontcolor="#1177BB"]
    mha   [label="Multi-Head Attention",     color="#228811", fontcolor="#228811"]
    add1  [label="+", shape=circle,          color="#999999", fontcolor="#999999", width=0.4, fixedsize=true]
    ln2   [label="Layer Norm 2",             color="#1177BB", fontcolor="#1177BB"]
    ffn   [label="Feed-Forward Network",     color="#CC8800", fontcolor="#CC8800"]
    add2  [label="+", shape=circle,          color="#999999", fontcolor="#999999", width=0.4, fixedsize=true]
    x_out [label="x  (output)",             shape=plaintext, style="", penwidth=1]

    x_in -> ln1 -> mha -> add1 -> ln2 -> ffn -> add2 -> x_out

    x_in -> add1 [style=dashed, color="#999999", label="residual", constraint=false]
    add1 -> add2 [style=dashed, color="#999999", label="residual", constraint=false]
}
```

@fig-ch10-matrix-residual shows the residual stream carrying information forward while gradients flow backward through additions.

```{dot}
//| label: fig-ch10-matrix-residual
//| fig-cap: "The residual stream — information flows unimpeded through addition, gradients flow backward."
digraph residual {
    rankdir=LR
    node [fontname="monospace", shape=box, style="rounded,filled", fillcolor=white, penwidth=2]
    edge [color="#555555"]

    x   [label="x",                shape=plaintext, style="", penwidth=1]
    sub [label="sublayer(x)",      color="#1177BB", fontcolor="#1177BB"]
    add [label="+", shape=circle,  color="#228811", fontcolor="#228811", width=0.4, fixedsize=true]
    out [label="x + sublayer(x)",  shape=plaintext, style="", penwidth=1]

    x -> sub -> add -> out
    x -> add [style=dashed, color="#999999", label="skip (x)", constraint=false]
}
```
